query_used: Condition: shoulder injury (rotator cuff tear or ligament damage) | Stage: Subacute | Symptoms: sharp pain during hand movements, throbbing pain during rest, numbness and tingling sensation in front and side of the shoulder, sometimes extending down to the arm | Onset: about two weeks after lifting a heavy object | Functional Limitations: extremely difficult to lift my arm, raise the shoulder, or perform normal activities | Red Flags: significant swelling (around 8-9 inches), spreading pain, numbness and tingling in the arm

retrieved_documents.content: the management of rotator common reason for a person with shoulder pain to be treated in the office. cuff injuries what are the risk factors for a rotator cuff tear? in general, there is a higher risk for a rotator cuff tear as we age. the most common time for being diagnosed is during the 40s and beyond because of the normal wear and tear to the tendons. in some cases, there is little to no pain caused by these tears. in younger patients, especially those that are part of overhead sports (tennis, baseball pitchers), tears can come from repeat trauma to the tendon from the force needed to play the sport. it is more common for a younger patient to get a tear from a specific injury such as a shoulder dislocation. how are rotator cuff tears diagnosed?
